metadata.title:
RECENT DEMONSTRATIONS
SHOW q. N. WILL CARRY
TRAINS NO LONGER, AND
ORDERS ARE PLACED FOR
NEW TIES.

pages.raw_text:
Employees of the Great Northern
railway state that the company is
at this late date making evtraor-
diTrfy efforts to secure ties and
men ito rebuild the wornout track
between here and the coast. A
number of small mills are employ
ed getting out ties to replace the
rotten ones which have been re-
sponsible for several bad wrecks
and great loss Of Property. AS the
latter loss directly affects divi-
dends, it, rather than danger to hui
man life, is very likely the incen-
tive of proposed repair work.


1t is not believed that any great
Improvement in the condition OF
the roadbed can be made this year,
as the cold weather will interior
with the work. Enough Of show
ing may be made, however, to blind
the public somewhat to the. danger
GUS condition of the road.
